title: All about WATER. Fluoride? Alkaline? Structured? Filtration? Contaminants in your spring water?

chunks.title: Introduction to Mood Enhancers
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces a product review from a nursing student who found dramatic improvements in her mental clarity after taking a supplement. She highlights the specific benefits she experienced, including a reduction in brain fog and improved mood.
chunks.transcript: this is not a placebo effect check out this review from elizabeth m on our mood memory and brain from heart and soil supplements she says i'm a nursing school college student who used to suffer from brain fog at times my thinking would be so cloudy that i felt like i was in slow motion it's difficult to explain i used to lack focus but guess what that changed by the third day of taking mood memory and brain from heart and soil supplements this is not a placebo effect i like to understand what i'm putting in my body and always test stuff out with a grain of salt in all honesty i wasn't expecting this great effect but wow now my kidney is clear and fog free i can't comprehend how i was functioning for animal organs side note this also helped my menstrual cramps and my mood is great i'm forever indebted to heart and soil test it on yourself reclaim your birthright to mental acuity

chunks.title: Importance of Quality Supplements
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the importance of sourcing high-quality animal-based supplements. They emphasize that their products are grass-fed and packaged in glass to avoid the pitfalls of plastic packaging.
chunks.transcript: i did a previous podcast on phosphatidylserine in bovine brain extract and benefits in terms of cognitive decline in aging it's real eating brain is a good thing for humans and it will improve your brain functions check out mood memory and brain from heart and soil all of our supplements are grass-fed grass finished and in glass bottles because that's the way you should do it plastic is garbage and we claim your birthright to optimal health check us out heart and soil dot co that is dot co and as a note there has never ever ever been a case never ever of bovines much informants epilaphaty prion disease in new zealand where we source all of our

chunks.title: The Fascination with Water Quality
chunks.summary: The speaker transitions to discussing water quality and filtration. He presents it as an important yet commonly overlooked aspect of health, addressing various filtering methods and the types of contaminants found in water.
chunks.transcript: pristine as the driven snow on this week's episode i wanted to talk about water and how to get the best water you can how to make water pristine as the driven snow i get a lot of questions what do you do for water how do you filter your water how do you do all of those things what filter are you using so i did a whole episode about it i hope it's interesting to you guys i find it fascinating people might say water is boring i think it's really interesting we talked about fluoride i talked about fluoride i myself we talked together about fluoride about structured water about alkaline water charlatan is chicanery uh we talked about bottled water contaminants radioactive elements in bottled water we talked about tanzania and fluorosis in tanzania and we talked about some other cool stuff in terms of how to filter your water is it berkey good is not good reverse osmosis should you re-mineralize all those things so i break it down for you a to z in this

chunks.title: Detailed Water Filtration Discussion
chunks.summary: This segment delves deeper into specific filtration techniques, including reverse osmosis and the drawbacks of common filter systems. The speaker critiques various products on the market, sharing personal testing experiences.

chunks.title: Fluoride in Drinking Water
chunks.summary: The potential health impacts of fluoride in drinking water are discussed. The speaker conveys their skepticism regarding water fluoridation and shares evidence linking fluoride to various health issues.

chunks.title: Fluoride and Dental Health
chunks.summary: The discussion continues with a focus on how fluoride may be inaccurately blamed for dental problems. The speaker promotes a nutrient-rich diet as a better preventive measure against cavities.

chunks.title: Alkaline Water and Structured Water Myths
chunks.summary: The speaker debunks the myths surrounding alkaline water and structured water, arguing that there's no scientific basis for their supposed benefits. He encourages the consumption of plain, clean water instead.

chunks.title: Concerns About Bottled Water
chunks.summary: This segment focuses on the hidden dangers of bottled water, including potential radioactive contaminants. The speaker discusses research suggesting that some popular bottled waters have harmful levels of radioactive elements.
chunks.transcript: the fluoride content of food so i thought okay how much fluoride is in meat this is a usda database and you can see that lamb has 0.32 parts per million so less than one which is what is in most drinking water chicken has 0.15 salami 0.41 beef beef carcass so animal foods do have fluoride in the meat now the question is then is this from fluoride in the drinking water of these animals so that's a question that is that is quite curious and i think it's going to be difficult to avoid fluoride in your meat but if you want to lower the fluoride in your diet you could drink fluoride free water again i think the best way to achieve that as i said in the beginning is drinking water from a from something like a reverse as much as filter so i want to show you fruit you can see that fruit is lower generally at least the ones that were studied in fluoride than animal foods so curious is the fruit watered with fluoride water probably but it doesn't seem to accumulate fluoride in the same way that animal meat does or perhaps there is a physiologic role for fluoride in animal meat and we're going to get some of it regardless of what we do now i mentioned earlier there was one study that showed a benefit to fluoride i just want to make sure that i show both sides of the issue the effects of treatment with fluoride on bone mineral density and fracture risk meta-analysis they say here fluoride treatment increases spine and hip bone mineral density depending on the treatment duration and also on the dose overall there was no effect on hip or spine fracture risk however in subgroup analyses a low fluoride dose are less than 20 milligrams per day of fluoride equivalence which is pretty low fluoride 20 milligrams a lot of fluoride if you if the oregon state university fluorides uh site says that four milligrams a day is the adequate intake 20 milligrams days are quite a big dose there was a significant reduction in fracture risk so i think it is also possible that in humans fluoride does serve a role again perhaps a u-shaped curve and that some fluoride is not harmful for us i think this is a really interesting question there is also a lot of discussion about fluoride and brain damage or i should say mental slowing in humans and if you look at that literature it's quite heterogeneous and the studies are not well conducted so i don't give vitamin e from animal fat we've talked about that a lot in the past so i think if you're going to get fluoride in your food let it occur naturally like our ancestors would have uh experienced and chunks.title: Final Thoughts on Water Quality
chunks.summary: The speaker wraps up the discussion on water by summarizing their approach to hydration, advocating for reverse osmosis as the best filtration method. They encourage listeners to understand what they're consuming and prioritize health.
